tier_justification: Tier 1 predicate: 2 work-units (typed emit channel in secretary.ts's runTurn; text-only forwarding in bridge/telegram-bridge.ts), above tier 0 because no existing test covers the goal state (Telegram replies free of tool echoes and the done footer), below tier 2 because <3 work-units and no irreversible/outward surface in the diff (the post-merge launchd restart is reversible ops).

evals.assert: Real runTurn (injected queryFn) classifies its output such that a text-only consumer receives the model's text but neither the tool echo (tool name in brackets) nor the '[Rachel] done turns=' footer; the probe's text-only collector stands in for the bridge, whose own filtering is independently checked by N2 criterion 2 and must be structural, not regex.

The terminal REPL still surfaces tool-use summaries and the done/cost footer (visibility unchanged for the terminal operator), while the Telegram bridge forwards only the model's text lines plus its own error line.
evals.verifier_instruction: Fresh clone: git clone -q --depth 1 -b fix/telegram-emit-channel https://github.com/blueman82/assistant-agent.git into a temp dir. Read rachel.ts and bridge/telegram-bridge.ts IN FULL. PASS only if (1) the terminal REPL path still receives/prints tool-use summary lines AND the done/cost footer (they must not have been deleted from runTurn, only classified), (2) the Telegram bridge's reply buffer collects ONLY the model's text output — tool summaries and the done footer must be excluded by a typed/structural mechanism, not by regex pattern-matching on line content, (3) the bridge's own '[secretary] error:' line still reaches the Telegram reply on a thrown turn. Return pass/fail per criterion with code excerpts as evidence. You receive only this instruction and the clone — no implementation conversation.

evals.assert: Fresh clone of the PR branch typechecks and passes the full test suite (typecheck and test as separate commands per the documented hang quirk).
evals.cmd:
set -e
d=$(mktemp -d)
git clone -q --depth 1 -b fix/telegram-emit-channel https://github.com/blueman82/assistant-agent.git "$d"
cd "$d"
npm install --silent >/dev/null 2>&1
npm run typecheck
npm test
evals.negative_control:
set -e
d=$(mktemp -d)
git clone -q --depth 1 -b fix/telegram-emit-channel https://github.com/blueman82/assistant-agent.git "$d"
cd "$d"
npm install --silent >/dev/null 2>&1
printf 'import { test } from "node:test";\nimport assert from "node:assert/strict";\ntest("neg control", () => { assert.equal(1, 2); });\n' > bridge/__neg.test.ts
if npm test >/dev/null 2>&1; then echo 'NEG CONTROL FAILED: suite passed with injected failing test'; exit 1; else echo 'negative control ok: suite fails when a failing test is injected'; fi

evals.assert: Message-loss guard: when runTurn produces only non-text lines (e.g. a turn that emitted nothing textual), the bridge still sends a reply (its '(no output)' fallback), never an empty sendMessage.
evals.cmd: set -e; d=$(mktemp -d); git clone -q --depth 1 -b fix/telegram-emit-channel https://github.com/blueman82/assistant-agent.git "$d"; cd "$d"; grep -n '(no output)' bridge/telegram-bridge.ts && echo 'N4 PASS: no-output fallback present on the filtered path'
evals.negative_control: if printf 'const replyText = buffer.join("");\nawait reply(replyText);' | grep -q '(no output)'; then echo 'NEG CONTROL FAILED'; exit 1; else echo 'negative control ok: grep detects a missing fallback'; fi

amendments.why: Upstream PR #15 (rachel rebrand) merged into main mid-flight and was reconciled into this branch: secretary.ts renamed to rachel.ts, env vars SECRETARY_* renamed to RACHEL_*, footer prefix now [Rachel]. Amendment retargets the probe's import path and fabricated env var names only; every assertion is unchanged (the 'done turns=' absence check already matches the rebranded footer). The negative control is deliberately NOT amended - it runs on pre-rename base 4a70688 where the old names are correct.
Same upstream rename: verifier must read rachel.ts (secretary.ts no longer exists at the PR head). Criteria unchanged.
